Article.ArticleTitle: Enhanced long-term oxidative remediation of calcium sulfate encapsulated KMnO4 for phenol-contaminated soil.

Abstract.AbstractText: To develop a controlled-release material for in-situ chemical oxidation (ISCO) in soil remediation, KMnO4 encapsulated by calcium sulfate (KMnO4@CaSO4) was prepared in this study via a rotary granulation process, which combines slow-release with enhanced oxidation capabilities. Characterization (XRD, FTIR, SEM-EDS) confirmed the successful physical encapsulation of KMnO4 within a hydrated CaSO4matrix without chemical reaction. The release lifetime of MnO4- from KMnO4@CaSO4 in the quartz sand column was approximately 17-fold longer than that of raw KMnO4. And the release kinetics of MnO4- were well described by a Fickian diffusion model (R2>0.9). MnO4- was sustainably released through the interconnected pore network generated by Ca2+ dissolution from the hydrated CaSO4 shell. In dynamic quartz sand column degradation tests, KMnO4@CaSO4 extended the complete phenol removal time 11-fold (60 h vs. 5 h) and increased the cumulative degradation by approximately 7-fold (54 mg vs. 6.7 mg), compared to raw KMnO4. Meanwhile, Ca2+ dissolved from KMnO4@CaSO4 enhanced the oxidation of phenol by MnO4-. Density Functional Theory calculations confirmed that Ca2+ coordination with phenol weakens the phenolic O-H bond and reduces the reaction energy barrier from 17.94 to -16.03 kcal mol-1. In soil column experiments, KMnO4@CaSO4 effectively suppressed the phenol rebound seen with raw KMnO4 and demonstrated sustained oxidative activity by maintaining phenol concentrations below the detection limit for over 56 h. After treatment, the soil properties (pH, organic matter, enzyme activities) recovered to uncontaminated baselines, and some bacteria (e.g., Pseudomonadota and Bacillota) were enriched.
